description.p: Dictée walks you through a plain-text list of sentences, recording each one from your microphone and producing matching WAV files together with a tab-separated transcript manifest.
The output is intended as a ready-to-use dataset for training or fine-tuning speech-recognition models: each audio file is 16-bit signed PCM, mono, at a 16 kHz sample rate.

screenshot.caption: Recording a sentence from the list

ul.li: Always-on microphone capture pipeline: the mic-startup transient happens once at app launch instead of on every recording.
Capture and DSP at 32-bit float / 32 kHz; downsample and quantize to S16LE / 16 kHz only at write time.
Per-clip DC-offset removal, peak normalisation to -1 dBFS, and a 15 ms linear fade-in/out.
Independent head and tail crop knobs to swallow Record / Stop key-press clicks.
Keyboard-first workflow with smart focus transitions between Previous, Record, Play, and Next.
